text.title: python-typing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for python-typing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using Any as a shortcut to silence mypy errors on unknown types instead of properly annotating the shape.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every Any annotation is a gap in type coverage. mypy treats Any as compatible with every type, meaning errors flow through silently. A function returning Any propagates the gap to all callers — one Any can suppress errors across an entire call chain.

failure-mode.description: Importing Any at module level and using it for dict payloads instead of defining TypedDict or Pydantic models.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Dict-typed returns (dict[str, Any]) are the most common Any sink. Replacing them with TypedDict definitions costs little and gives mypy visibility into key access patterns.

failure-mode.description: Adding # type: ignore without a specific error code, e.g., # type: ignore instead of # type: ignore[attr-defined].
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bare # type: ignore silences all mypy errors on the line, including future ones unrelated to the original issue. A scoped code silences only the specific error class. warn_unused_ignores = true in mypy config flags stale ignores when the underlying issue is fixed.

failure-mode.description: Using cast(SomeType, value) to satisfy mypy instead of adding a proper narrowing guard (isinstance, assert, or TypeIs function).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: cast() is a lie to the type checker — it makes no runtime assertion. If the actual type differs, the bug propagates silently. A narrowing guard (if isinstance(x, SomeType): ...) is both a type assertion and a runtime safety check.

failure-mode.description: Mixing Optional[X] and X | None in the same codebase, or mixing Union[X, Y] and X | Y.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mixed styles produce inconsistent grep results and confuse ruff --fix idempotency. Pick X | None / X | Y for all new code and enforce with ruff UP007. Existing Optional imports can be batch-migrated with ruff check --select UP --fix.

failure-mode.description: Applying @runtime_checkable to a Protocol that is checked with isinstance() in a tight loop or frequently-called middleware.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: isinstance(obj, SomeProtocol) with @runtime_checkable checks only method presence, not signatures, and does so on every call. The overhead is measurable on hot paths. Use it only when the check is infrequent and the dynamic dispatch is genuinely needed.
